mutation: composite: goal is value-maximizing acceptance of ships into a tidal anchorage (open-ended selection), with output tightened so a valid schedule must jointly satisfy heterogeneous-radii swing-circle packing, a small discrete tide-window entry/exit grid, and time-space continuity (fixed anchor point held for the whole stay, non-overlap only enforced across shared epochs) -- the composition is what makes optimal construction NP-hard, not any single mechanism.
theme: ships swinging at anchor in a crowded bay
title: Slack Water: Scheduling Ships at a Tidal Anchorage
strategies: trivial: accept only the single best individually-feasible ship (the checker's own baseline construction)
greedy: arrival-order processing, maximal (literal arr..dep) tick span, first-fit spiral-from-centre placement -- no reuse planning

strong: epoch-graph reformulation -- value-descending processing order plus minimal-span tick-pair search (few epochs -> cheap to enumerate), so scarce high-value ships claim their slot before cheap flexible ships squat on it for the whole horizon; repair pass for leftovers
invalid: entry tick == exit tick and an out-of-bounds anchor point, must score 0 on every test
